summary: Parallel review: FAIL (2 critical, 8 warning, 10 suggestion). Compound gate: PASS

findingsList.text: 🟡 `bin/lib/extensions.mjs:212` — `runExecuteRun` duplicates the timeout + circuit-breaker pattern instead of using `callHook`; extract a shared `invokeWithProtection` helper in v2 to prevent logic drift

🟡 `bin/lib/extensions.mjs:129` — Shallow spread `{ ...ctx }` in `callHook` shares nested mutable objects (e.g., `findings` array) by reference; a buggy extension could mutate the caller's data; consider `structuredClone` or documenting context as read-only
🟡 `tasks/task-24/handshake.json:3` — nodeType "build" with empty artifacts would fail the project's own `validateHandshake()`; should be nodeType "gate" for a verification task
🟡 `examples/extensions/log-phases.mjs:22` — Shell metacharacter injection via `${ctx.taskTitle}` interpolation into `sh -c` command; user-authored titles could execute arbitrary commands

🟡 `.team/features/extension-system/tasks/task-24/handshake.json:9` — No test-output.txt artifact was saved; `"artifacts": []` means there is no persistent evidence trail. Future verification tasks should capture output as an artifact.

🟡 `test/oscillation-ticks.test.mjs:302` — Intermittently flaky test ("halts feature with exit code 1 after 3 reps of K=2 pattern") not flagged by the builder. Not caused by extension-system changes (the `transition` code path was not modified), but observed during review. File as backlog item.
🔴 `.team/features/extension-system/tasks/task-24/handshake.json:6` — Handshake claims 0 failures but actual result is 2 failures; update summary to reflect actual test results
🔴 `test/e2e.test.mjs:260` — "finalize" test fails with `spawnSync node ENOENT` on feature branch but passes on main; investigate whether async changes to `buildReviewBrief` caused regression
🟡 `.team/features/extension-system/tasks/task-24/artifacts/` — No test-output.txt saved; save actual `npm test` output so claims are verifiable
🟡 `examples/extensions/log-phases.mjs:22` — Shell metacharacter injection via `ctx.taskTitle` interpolation into command string; use array-form spawn or shell-escape the value
